qa_pairs.question: How can a user view available commands and their details within the OpenStack command-line interface?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can view available commands categorized by groups by typing `help` at the OpenStack prompt. To see commands belonging to a specific command group, type `help <command group>`. For details on a particular command, including its options and arguments, type `help <command group> <command>`.

qa_pairs.question: Why are OpenStack CLI commands grouped by category in the documentation, especially considering administrator access?
qa_pairs.answer: OpenStack CLI commands are grouped by category in the documentation because many commands are only available to OpenStack administrators and would return an error if a non-administrator tried to use them. These lists help average users easily identify which commands are available for their use.
